page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.medium is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a single dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor core running at 2.8 GHz with 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the single-core architecture delivers specialized performance characteristics. Single-threaded tasks show notable efficiency, with bzip3 decompression reaching 14.17 GB/sec, categorized as strong, and gzip compression achieving an average speed of 29.99 GB/sec. However, due to the single-core limitation, multi-core stress-ng benchmarks and PassMark CPU scores are low. Memory bandwidth is measured at 115.7 GB/sec for cached reads and 30.4 GB/sec for uncached reads, while write speeds reach 12.07 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are relatively constrained, with Redis handling 2.4 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gn.medium offers a low-entry-cost profile for ARM64-native development, testing, and lightweight compute workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution without the CPU credit exhaustion risks of burstable instances, though the 2.0 GB memory capacity limits its use for memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, microservices, edge routing, static web serving, and basic CI/CD pipeline tasks that do not require high multi-threaded throughput or large memory footprints.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.medium is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor core running at 2.8 GHz. It features 2.0 GB of system memory, a 36 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded performance in bzip3 decompression (14.17 GB/sec) and average gzip compression, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are low due to the single vCPU configuration. This instance provides a highly cost-efficient entry point for ARM64 development, lightweight single-threaded applications, microservices, and static web serving where consistent, dedicated CPU performance is required without the overhead of larger multi-core configurations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring a single dedicated 2.8 GHz Graviton Neoverse-V2 core, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with one dedicated 2.8 GHz Graviton core, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated single-core ARM64 compute instance offering consistent performance and high network efficiency for lightweight workloads and microservices.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 core running at 2.8 GHz.
2.0 GB of system memory with a compute-optimized 1:2 resource ratio.
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Strong single-threaded performance in bzip3 decompression benchmarks.
No local storage or GPU hardware included.
Best suited for lightweight microservices, static web serving, and ARM64 development.
